tluaMetatableOperators.tlua(33,24): error TLUA2322: Type 'string' is not assignable to type 'number'.
tluaMetatableOperators.tlua(37,17): error TLUA2322: Type 'number' is not assignable to type '{ x: number; }'.
tluaMetatableOperators.tlua(37,21): error TLUA2322: Type '{ x: number; }' is not assignable to type 'number'.
tluaMetatableOperators.tlua(43,15): error TLUA2365: Operator '+' cannot be applied to types '{ n: number; }' and '{ n: number; }'.
tluaMetatableOperators.tlua(46,22): error TLUA100028: An arithmetic operand must be of type 'any' or 'number'.
tluaMetatableOperators.tlua(47,21): error TLUA100028: An arithmetic operand must be of type 'any' or 'number'.


==== tluaMetatableOperators.tlua (6 errors) ====
    // An operator on a paired operand dispatches to the metamethod its metatable commits to. The
    // metamethod's return type is the expression's type; its parameters check the operands.
    
    local Ops = {
      __add = function(a: { x: number }, b: { x: number } | number): { x: number } return a; end,
      __mul = function(a: { x: number }, s: number): (number, string) return a.x * s, "w"; end,
      __lt = function(a: { x: number }, b: { x: number }): boolean return a.x < b.x; end,
      __le = function(a: { x: number }, b: { x: number }): boolean return a.x <= b.x; end,
      __eq = function(a: object, b: object): boolean return true; end,
      __unm = function(a: { x: number }): { x: number } return a; end,
      __pow = function(a: { x: number }, n: number): { x: number } return a; end,
    };
    
    local v = setmetatable({ x = 1 }, Ops);
    local w = setmetatable({ x = 2 }, Ops);
    
    local sum: { x: number } = v + w;
    local first: number = v * 2;        // a multi-return metamethod adjusts to its first value
    local powed: { x: number } = v ^ 2; // ^ is Lua's __pow
    local neg: { x: number } = -v;      // unary minus is __unm
    local lt: boolean = v < w;
    local gt: boolean = v > w;          // a > b runs __lt(b, a)
    local le: boolean = v <= w;
    local ge: boolean = v >= w;
    local eq: boolean = v == w;         // the no-overlap error is suppressed
    local ne: boolean = v ~= w;
    
    // A compound assignment runs the same metamethod its operator does and checks the result back
    // into the target.
    v += w;
    
    // An operand the metamethod's parameter does not admit is an error.
    local badOperand = v * "s";
                           ~~~
!!! error TLUA2322: Type 'string' is not assignable to type 'number'.
    
    // A handler found on the right operand still receives the left as its first argument: `2 ^ v`
    // checks 2 against __pow's { x: number } first parameter and v against its number second.
    local badLeft = 2 ^ v;
                    ~
!!! error TLUA2322: Type 'number' is not assignable to type '{ x: number; }'.
                        ~
!!! error TLUA2322: Type '{ x: number; }' is not assignable to type 'number'.
    
    // A pairing that commits only to __eq makes its tables equality-comparable, but an operator it
    // has no handler for is still an error. `+` reports that its own way.
    local eqOnly = setmetatable({ n = 1 }, { __eq = function(a: object, b: object): boolean return true; end });
    local eqOk: boolean = eqOnly == eqOnly;
    local noAdd = eqOnly + eqOnly;
                  ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA2365: Operator '+' cannot be applied to types '{ n: number; }' and '{ n: number; }'.
    
    // Unary minus is arithmetic now: a non-numeric operand is rejected whether or not it is paired.
    local minusString = -"abc";
                         ~~~~~
!!! error TLUA100028: An arithmetic operand must be of type 'any' or 'number'.
    local minusTable = -eqOnly;
                        ~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA100028: An arithmetic operand must be of type 'any' or 'number'.
